summary: Russia announced a nationwide alert due to the launch of a MiG-31K carrying an X-47M2 Kinzhal missile. Ukrainian forces reported multiple UAV incursions across several oblasts and a Russian‑controlled advance into villages in Dnipropetrovsk, Kharkiv, and Dnipropetrovsk regions. A large air defense effort claimed the downing of 217 hostile UAVs while Russia prepared a mass missile strike with Tu‑95MS and Tu‑160 bombers.
key_points: Russia launched a MiG-31K fighter equipped with an X-47M2 Kinzhal aerobalistic missile, prompting a nationwide alert (Source: @eRadarrua/94100).
Ukrainian air defenses reported shooting down 217 hostile UAVs, including Shahed, Gerbera, and Italmas types, across northern, southern and eastern sectors (Source: @kpszsu/63176).
Russian forces took control of the villages Lesnoe and Novopodgorodnoe in Dnipropetrovsk region and Karaichne and Budarki in Kharkiv region (Source: @tass_agency/378054).
A Russian drone struck a multi‑storey building in Galaţi, Romania, causing a fire, two injuries and evacuation of 70 residents (Source: @UkraineNow/81524).

Ukrainian reports indicated UAV activity near Snovsk, Sumy, Chernihiv, Odesa, and Zaporizhzhia, with several drones heading toward specific towns (Source: @eRadarrua/94111, @kpszsu/63173, @kpszsu/63149, @kpszsu/63169).
A Russian missile attack using an Iskander-M ballistic missile from Kursk region was recorded, alongside 232 strike UAVs targeting 14 locations (Source: @kpszsu/63176).
Ukrainian forces reported a Russian drone destroying a school in Novhorod‑Siverskyi district, with no casualties reported (Source: @UkraineNow/81526).

sections.executive_summary: Russia launched a MiG-31K carrying a Kinzhal missile, triggering a nationwide alert. Ukrainian air defenses downed 217 hostile UAVs while Russia prepared a mass missile strike with Tu‑95MS and Tu‑160 bombers. Russian forces seized villages in Dnipropetrovsk and Kharkiv regions and conducted cross‑border drone attacks.

points: Ukrainian air defense units reported shooting down 217 hostile UAVs of various types, including Shahed, Gerbera, and Italmas, across northern, southern and eastern sectors, indicating a high‑intensity UAV threat (Source: @kpszsu/63176).

Ukrainian authorities documented multiple UAV incursions: three near Snovich, four near Terna in Sumy, and several drones heading toward Cherkasy, Zaporizhzhia, and Odesa, highlighting widespread aerial reconnaissance and strike activity (Source: @eRadarrua/94111, @kpszsu/63173, @kpszsu/63166).
A Russian drone destroyed a school in the Novhorod‑Siverskyi district of Chernihiv region, burning the building and its contents, though no casualties were reported (Source: @UkraineNow/81526).

Ukrainian officials reported a Russian drone strike on a multi‑storey residential building in Galaţi, Romania, causing a fire, two injuries and the evacuation of 70 residents, underscoring cross‑border threats (Source: @UkraineNow/81524).
Ukrainian leadership highlighted a critical shortage of air‑defence systems, urging the United States for licenses to produce Patriot‑compatible PAC‑3 missiles (Source: @UkraineNow/81517).
Russia announced the launch of a MiG-31K fighter equipped with an X-47M2 Kinzhal aerobalistic missile, prompting a nationwide alert across Ukraine (Source: @eRadarrua/94100).
The Russian Ministry of Defence reported that 208 Ukrainian UAVs were shot down over Russian regions during the night (Source: @tass_agency/378024).

Russian forces seized control of the villages Lesnoe and Novopodgorodnoe in Dnipropetrovsk region and Karaichne and Budarki in Kharkiv region, expanding ground footholds (Source: @tass_agency/378054).

Russia prepared a mass missile strike involving six Tu‑95MS bombers from Olenya and Engels airfields and two Tu‑160 bombers from the Ukrainianka airfield, with up to 40 X‑101 cruise missiles ready (Source: @eRadarrua/94134).
A Russian Iskander‑M ballistic missile was launched from Kursk region, accompanied by 232 strike UAVs targeting 14 locations across Ukraine (Source: @kpszsu/63176).
UAV activity was reported near Snovich in the Sumy region, with three drones observed flying past the town (Source: @eRadarrua/94111).
Ukrainian defenses recorded UAVs approaching from the north of Sumy heading toward Terna, indicating pressure on the Sumy‑Chernihiv axis (Source: @kpszsu/63173).
Russian forces reported control of villages in Kharkiv region (Karaichne, Budarki), potentially affecting eastern supply lines (Source: @tass_agency/378054).
A Russian Iskander‑M ballistic missile launch from Kursk targeted eastern sectors, accompanied by numerous strike UAVs (Source: @kpszsu/63176).
Ukrainian air defenses downed multiple UAVs over eastern oblasts, including Shahed and Italmas types (Source: @kpszsu/63176).
UAVs were observed heading from the north of Dnipropetrovsk region toward Cherkasy, indicating a northern approach vector (Source: @kpszsu/63152).
UAVs from the north of Sumy region were tracked moving toward Talalayivka, showing continued northern incursions (Source: @kpszsu/63166).
A Russian drone was reported flying from the north of Zaporizhzhia region toward Zaporizhzhia city (Source: @kpszsu/63169).
Ukrainian forces claimed to have downed 217 hostile UAVs across the northern sector, reflecting intense aerial combat (Source: @kpszsu/63176).
Russian MiG‑31K launch was noted nationwide, affecting northern airspace monitoring (Source: @eRadarrua/94100).
Four UAVs were reported operating from the sea near the Black Sea, heading toward the city of Chornomorsk (Source: @eRadarrua/94112).
Ukrainian reports indicated UAV activity near Odesa, with five drones observed from the sea heading toward Tatarbunary (Source: @eRadarrua/94114).
A Russian drone strike on a residential building in Galaţi, Romania, originated from attacks directed at Odesa, showing southern cross‑border reach (Source: @UkraineNow/81524).
Ukrainian air defenses downed numerous UAVs over southern sectors, part of the 217 total claimed (Source: @kpszsu/63176).
Russian Iskander‑M missile launch and accompanying UAV swarm targeted southern locations among the 14 hit sites (Source: @kpszsu/63176).
Ukrainian air defenses reported shooting down 217 hostile UAVs of various types across the country, indicating a high‑intensity air‑defence effort (Source: @kpszsu/63176).
The Russian Ministry of Defence claimed 208 Ukrainian UAVs were downed over Russian territory during a single night (Source: @tass_agency/378024).
Russia prepared a large‑scale missile strike with six Tu‑95MS and two Tu‑160 bombers, each potentially carrying up to 40 X‑101 cruise missiles (Source: @eRadarrua/94134).
A Russian Iskander‑M ballistic missile launch from Kursk was accompanied by 232 strike UAVs targeting multiple Ukrainian locations (Source: @kpszsu/63176).

Multiple UAV incursions were reported across Ukrainian oblasts, including Sumy, Chernihiv, Odesa, and Zaporizhzhia, highlighting widespread aerial pressure (Source: @eRadarrua/94111, @kpszsu/63173, @kpszsu/63169).

key_developments: Russia launched a MiG-31K fighter equipped with an X-47M2 Kinzhal missile, prompting a nationwide alert across Ukraine (Source: @eRadarrua/94100).
Ukrainian air defenses downed 217 hostile UAVs, including Shahed, Gerbera, and Italmas types, across northern, southern and eastern sectors (Source: @kpszsu/63176).
Russian forces seized control of the villages Lesnoe and Novopodgorodnoe in Dnipropetrovsk region and Karaichne and Budarki in Kharkiv region (Source: @tass_agency/378054).
A Russian drone struck a multi‑storey residential building in Galaţi, Romania, causing a fire, two injuries and the evacuation of 70 residents (Source: @UkraineNow/81524).

Russia prepared a mass missile strike with six Tu‑95MS bombers from Olenya and Engels and two Tu‑160 bombers from Ukrainianka, ready to launch up to 40 X‑101 cruise missiles (Source: @eRadarrua/94134).
A Russian Iskander‑M ballistic missile was launched from Kursk region, accompanied by 232 strike UAVs targeting 14 locations across Ukraine (Source: @kpszsu/63176).
Ukrainian authorities reported a Russian drone destroying a school in Novhorod‑Siverskyi district, Chernihiv region, with no casualties (Source: @UkraineNow/81526).
sections.threat_assessment: Russian aerial capabilities remain a significant threat, demonstrated by the MiG‑31K launch, mass bomber preparations, and extensive UAV and missile attacks across multiple fronts. Ukrainian air defenses are actively engaging, achieving high UAV attrition, but the volume and variety of threats, including ballistic missiles and cross‑border drone strikes, strain defensive resources. Continued Russian ground advances in Dnipropetrovsk and Kharkiv regions could facilitate deeper penetration and logistical support for air operations.
sections.regional_response: Ukraine is intensifying air‑defence operations and seeking additional Western air‑defence assets, as highlighted by President Zelensky's appeals for Patriot‑compatible missile production licenses. Neighboring states, notably Romania, have experienced direct attacks, prompting heightened border security and diplomatic protests. International partners are monitoring the escalation, with some, like Sweden, pledging fighter deliveries, while others remain cautious.
sections.intelligence_notes: Monitoring of MiG‑31K flight paths and Kinzhal launch signatures is critical for early warning. UAV tracking data shows recurring ingress routes from the north, east, and sea, suggesting predictable launch corridors that can be targeted for interdiction. The seizure of villages in Dnipropetrovsk and Kharkiv may serve as staging areas for future air‑ground coordination, requiring close surveillance of logistics and command nodes.
